weather.summary: Saturday: 15°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 10°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend! I've found a mix of exciting activities in and around Hackney, from exploring city farms to enjoying free museum events. Plus, there are some great options for booking ahead for Easter adventures!

activities.title: Camden Market Easter Weekender
activities.description: Enjoy a free Easter Weekender with an Easter art trail, face painting, and bonnet making. Soak up the atmosphere and street performances. Spend only if you decide to eat or shop.
activities.whyItWorks: The free Easter trail and activities are perfect for a 3-year-old, offering visual stimulation and creative fun in a bustling environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road or the bus routes 24, 27, 29, or 31.
transport.tip: Camden can get crowded, so consider going earlier in the day.

activities.title: Two Weeks of Easter Activities
activities.description: Victoria Park hosts a fortnight of free, daily activities, including sports and fishing. There's a break for the Easter weekend, but otherwise, there are one to two major activities a day.
activities.whyItWorks: With a variety of activities, including outdoor sports, there's something to capture the attention of a 3-year-old. The park's open space is great for running around.

transport.fromHackney: Take bus routes 277, 425, or D6 to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for a schedule and details of start points.

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: A reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A calm environment for toddlers to explore books and stories.

transport.fromHackney: Located within Hackney Downs Studios, easily accessible by bus or train to Hackney Downs station.
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Discover London's history as a port with child-friendly exhibits and hands-on activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed with children in mind, featuring activity sheets and hands-on activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Canary Wharf.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Sound Explorers at Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive sessions exploring sound and music.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and sensory experience suitable for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check for accessibility if traveling with a pushchair as Bethnal Green station may not be accessible.

activities.title: Animal Walk at the Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Get up close with farm animals in the heart of Hackney.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance for a 3-year-old to see and interact with animals, a fun and educational experience.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney, easily accessible by bus or walking.
transport.tip: Check the farm's website for any special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A sweet pond-themed wooden playground.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for toddlers, offering a safe and stimulating play environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford or the Overground to Hackney Wick.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Library Time at Hackney Libraries
activities.description: Storytime and activities for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and engaging way to encourage a love of reading.

transport.fromHackney: Check the Hackney Council website for your nearest library and transport options.
transport.tip: Perfect for a rainy day.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Easter Family Festival
activities.description: Participate in free family-friendly activities and learn from scientists. See a person-sized T. Rex and enjoy LEGO Super Builder sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs and LEGOs are always a hit! The interactive exhibits cater to young children's curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Tube: District, Circle or Piccadilly line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid crowds, especially during the festival.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits, including the Wonderlab (ticketed) and space-themed activities. The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play. The Pattern Pod is great for light exploration, sensory and interactive fun.
activities.whyItWorks: The interactive exhibits are great for curious minds. The Garden and Pattern Pod are specifically designed for younger children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Tube: District, Circle or Piccadilly line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Fun
activities.description: Enjoy free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts. A must for families and animal-lovers, with a host of goats, sheep, and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal encounters and creative crafts are perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Located in Zone 1, making it easily accessible.

activities.title: British Library
activities.description: Explore the world of fairy tales at the British Library with the new free show Fairy Tales, which opens just in time for Easter. The Library also has a 'Story Explorers' program for children.
activities.whyItWorks: An immersive journey through the world of fairy tales with exciting works from the Library's collection.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Euston or the bus routes 73, 205, or 476.
transport.tip: Booking online is required but there is plenty of availability throughout the holidays

activities.title: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun
activities.description: Enjoy shows, dancing, puppetry, and free events this Easter. Visit the Giant Chalkboard, WordPlay sessions, and Architecture Explorers Family Trail.
activities.whyItWorks: A wide range of activities suitable for young children, with many free options.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then the Central Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for a detailed schedule and booking information.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore the museum's exhibits, including stuffed animals and musical instruments, and enjoy the gardens with views over London.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum is manageable for little children, and the gardens offer space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check out the Animal Walk and Butterfly House.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Visit a small zoo with rare species of birds and animals. See wallabies, lemurs, and Scottish wildcats.
activities.whyItWorks: A free zoo experience that's perfect for a short visit with a toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Northern Line to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Enjoy a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides open space and multiple activities to keep a toddler entertained.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check for events happening in the park.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: A family Easter adventure with a themed egg trail, indoor and outdoor play, and animal encounters.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines physical activity with animal interactions, offering a full day of fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Tube: Piccadilly Line to Hounslow West, then a bus to Hobbledown Heath.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance for a discount.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An exciting outdoor activity with a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Hampton Court from Waterloo station.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time.

activities.title: Spring into Wild Fangs
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London, SE23 3PQ
activities.description: In celebration of spring, discover the life cycles of real-life creatures.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands on learning and educational oportunity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Check the Horniman Website for tickets

activities.title: Schiaparelli: Fashion Becomes Art
activities.description: Explore the first, groundbreaking designs by Schiaparelli, through to the major fashion house's present-day incarnation in the hands of its creative director, Daniel Roseberry.
activities.whyItWorks: Bright, bold, and interesting designs to grab attention.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Tube: District, Circle or Piccadilly line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check V&A Website for tours.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends at Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore how the iconic characters are brought to life. Ticket gives unlimited visits.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: Join Paddington on a marmalade-filled adventure in this musical adaptation of the beloved book.
activities.whyItWorks: A visually appealing and engaging show for young children, based on a familiar character.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Covent Garden or Embankment Station.
transport.tip: Pack marmalade sandwiches for the little one!

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for giant Easter eggs hidden around the zoo and uncover a secret code. Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys, and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with an Easter egg hunt, making it an exciting day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus routes 274 or 31 to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Easter Quest at Eltham Palace and Gardens
activities.price: Paid, in addition to paid admission
activities.description: Embark on a fun-filled adventure quest around the house and gardens. Follow the Easter trail, hunt for eggs, and answer questions to earn a chocolate reward.
activities.whyItWorks: A mix of history, outdoor exploration, and a chocolate treat, perfect for a family day out.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Eltham station, then a short walk to the palace.
transport.tip: Check the weather forecast and dress accordingly.
